scenarios.description: throws IllegalArgumentException when visitor is null (Validate.notNull(visitor))
scenarios.precondition: visitor == null, root is dummy Node; using real Validate
pseudocode: NodeVisitor visitor = null; Node root = new Element("div");

scenarios.description: throws IllegalArgumentException when root is null (Validate.notNull(root))
scenarios.precondition: visitor is no-op, root == null; using real Validate

scenarios.description: single-node tree invokes head and tail once at depth 0 (no children)
scenarios.precondition: visitor records calls into list; root has zero children
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor.head called once with (root,0) then visitor.tail once with (root,0)

scenarios.description: two-level tree descends into child and ascends back producing correct head/tail order
scenarios.precondition: root has exactly one child; visitor logs head/tail sequence
scenarios.expectedResult: events sequence: head(root,0), head(child,1), tail(child,1), tail(root,0)
pseudocode:
Element root = new Element("div"); Element child = new Element("p"); root.appendChild(child);
    List<String> events = []; NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d)->events.add((n instanceof Element?n.nodeName():"")+":"+d);

scenarios.description: node removal in head causes skip of tail for removed node and continues siblings
scenarios.precondition: visitor.head removes current node; root has two children; using real Node.remove()
scenarios.expectedResult: removed childâ€™s tail is not called; traversal visits remaining sibling
pseudocode:
Element root = new Element("div"); Element a=new Element("a"), b=new Element("b"); root.appendChild(a); root.appendChild(b);
    NodeVisitor visitor = new NodeVisitor(){ List<String> events=new ArrayList<>(); public void head(Node n,int d){ events.add("H:"+n.nodeName()); if(n.nodeName().equals("a")) n.remove(); } public void tail(Node n,int d){ events.add("T:"+n.nodeName()); } };

scenarios.description: multiple siblings with depth 0 iterates each sibling correctly
scenarios.precondition: root has three children; visitor logs node order
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor.head/tail called for each sibling in order
pseudocode:
Element root = new Element("div"); for(String n:Arrays.asList("a","b","c")) root.appendChild(new Element(n));
    List<String> events = new ArrayList<>(); NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d)->events.add(n.nodeName());

scenarios.description: Elements overload with empty list does nothing
scenarios.precondition: visitor no-op; elements is empty Elements
scenarios.expectedResult: no exception thrown; no side effects
pseudocode: NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d)->{}; Elements elements = new Elements();

scenarios.description: Elements overload with multiple elements iterates each independently
scenarios.precondition: elements contains two elements; visitor logs heads
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor.head and tail called for each element subtree in order
pseudocode:
Element e1=new Element("div"), e2=new Element("p"); Elements elements=new Elements(e1,e2);
    List<String> events = new ArrayList<>(); NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d)->events.add(n.nodeName());
